source: D58685-Rev04-HAL4.0 Traceability Plan.pdf
title: 2. Requirements 需求
text_content: Per procedure INC02, all medical devices need to have a Traceability Plan in place which describes the traceability requirements for the components of the device.  Any future changes to the product require that this plan be reviewed and updated to comply with the product’s related Critical To Safety and Critical To Reliability lists.  The approved Traceability Plan shall be stored in the Device Master Record (DMR). 根据INC02 程序，所有医疗设备都需要有可追溯性计划，该计划描述了设备组件的可追溯性要求。 未来 对产品的任何更改都要求对该计划进行审查和更新，以符合产品相关的关键安全和关键可靠性列表。 经 批准的追溯计划应存储在设备主记录(DMR)中.

source: D59555 HAL 4.0 Risk Management Plan rev03.pdf
title: 7.0 Verification Activities > 7.1 Risk control measures
text_content: The three risk control options shall be considered in their order of priority: o Inherently safe design and manufacture; o Protective measures in the device itself or in the manufacturing process; o Information for safety (warnings/precautions/contra-indications) and, where appropriate, training to users. All recognized risk control measures shall be implemented to reduce risk to or maintain risk at an acceptable level. Implementation of risk control measures shall continue until successive risk control options or measures do not further reduce the estimated risk. Whenever possible, requirements of international standards or their corresponding harmonized versions shall be utilized as risk control measures. These applied standards are listed or referenced in Appendix B of this RMP. The implementation of risk control measures shall be verified. The effectiveness of the implemented risk control measures shall be verified or, as appropriate, validated.

text_content: Existing HAL test cases will be selected to run based on the impact analysis with regard to the changes made in HAL 4.0 MR1 including 4.0 MR1 development, DR fixes, merge with True Beam, and compatibility with updates to ARIA and Eclipse. All safety and risk controls will be tested as a part of verification unless justification is provided. Impact analysis for determining the verification Test Case and Test Description was performed via D71770 HAL 4.0 MR1 Verification TRR1 Impact Analysis [13] and D73115 HAL 4.0 MR1 Verification TRR2 Impact Analysis [14]. Regression analysis will be performed based on an opt in methodology, whereby requirements affected by HAL 4.0 MR1 will be identified along with a justification why regression testing is necessary. Linked test cases to those requirements will then be included in the HAL 4.0 MR1 regression test suite: all other HAL test cases will not be run for the HAL 4.0 MR1 release.

text_content: For HAL4.0 Function to be tested Simulate common failure cases and verify tooling can detect such problems. Req. / Use Case / Hazard Number / DR User needs U1 in section 2.2 # Test Input Test Output Block X1 motor motion by hand, simulate a 5. failure case of test, monitor the change of current value, whether the tooling can identify failure and cut off motor power automatically. Repeat this test for all five axes. When X1 motor start moving, use hand to block X1 blade. The motor will stop rotating very quick. Meanwhile, Amperemeter A show that current value is momentary increasing from 30mA to 100mA (it means out of measuring range) for about 100ms. X2, Y1, Y2, F axes get the same results. Disconnect X1 linear encoder cable, check 6. the encoder value on monitor. It should be 0. Repeat this test for all five axes. X1 shows 0. X2, Y1, Y2, F axes get the same results. title: 2. Requirements 需求
text_content: Per procedure INC02, all medical devices need to have a Traceability Plan in place which describes the traceability requirements for the components of the device.  Any future changes to the product require that this plan be reviewed and updated to comply with the product’s related Critical To Safety and Critical To Reliability lists.  The approved Traceability Plan shall be stored in the Device Master Record (DMR). 根据INC02 程序，所有医疗设备都需要有可追溯性计划，该计划描述了设备组件的可追溯性要求。 未来 对产品的任何更改都要求对该计划进行审查和更新，以符合产品相关的关键安全和关键可靠性列表。 经 批准的追溯计划应存储在设备主记录(DMR)中.

source: D44094_HAL4.0_CER_Rev05.pdf
title: 3. DAEN > 7.1.8 Conclusions of the Analysis of Manufacturer Data (Continued)
text_content: Mitigations through product design and labeling are in place. The mitigations were derived from thorough risk analysis processes through which risk control measures are formulated, implemented, and validated during product development. The risk control measures have been tested through verification and validation activities, and through product usability studies. The risks and their associated potential hazards have been assessed via analysis of available clinical data regarding the use of HAL. The safety findings in the clinical data were analyzed in context of the benefits to the patients as described above. Clinical data was analyzed for any evidence of the residual risks of HAL 4.0 identified in the RMR report. No adverse events or clinical toxicities were reported as attributable to the residual risks outlined in Section 5.1.4 (e.g. radiation over/under exposure, collision).

source: D65245 HAL 4.0 Packaging Validation Protocol and Report Rev 04.docx.pdf
title: 10. Summary of Test Results
text_content: The packages maintained integrity and the devices passed visual and functionality testing, therefore the packaging is validated and approved for production usage. Full test results are attached. The 3-Phase Isolation Transformer crate showed minor damage to wooden support blocks that surround the bracket at the base of the transformer. There was no damage to the transformer and functionality was maintained. Given the extreme nature of the testing which is outside what is expected to be seen in our real-world distribution environment, it is acceptable and expected that there would be minor damage. The packaging is expected to provide sacrificial protection to the product. The transformer did not move such that it would come in contact with the walls of the crate or out of the straps that secure it, therefore the wooden support blocks performed their function, no changes are required to the design, and the packaging is considered validated. Figure 1 shows the damage to the wooden blocks.

title: 2. Definitions
text_content: 2.1. Validation means confirmation by examination and provision of objective evidence that the particular requirements for a specific intended use can be consistently fulfilled. 21CFR Part 820.3 (z) 2.2. Process validation means establishing by objective evidence that a process consistently produces a result or product meeting its predetermined specifications. 21CFR Part 820.3 (z) (1) 2.3. Design validation means establishing by objective evidence that device specifications conform to user needs and intended use(s). 21CFR Part 820.3 (z) (2) 2.4. Verification means confirmation by examination and provision of objective evidence that specified requirements have been fulfilled. 21CFR Part 820.3 (z) (2) (aa) 2.5. Test Protocol: A detailed set of instructions/plan for conduct of verification and validation in the current context. 2.6. Test Case: A document specifying inputs, predicted results, and a set of execution conditions for a test item (IEEE 829 Standard)
